parameters.name: ambdiff
parameters.label: Ambient uses Diffuse
parameters.description: Uses the Diffuse parameter for Ambient when checked.

parameters.name: shininess
parameters.label: Shininess
parameters.description: Controls the specular highlights (glossyness) of an object. Higher settings are more glossy, like plastic or shiny metal. Lower settings give more of a matte finish.

parameters.name: colormap
parameters.label: Color Map
parameters.description: Specifies a texture that is multiplied by the results of all of the lighting calculations. The alpha of this map is used as a part of calculating the objects alpha.  Clicking on the arrows to the righ

parameters.name: texturesamplingmode
parameters.label: Texture Sampling Mode
parameters.description: Mode to pick how the texture is to be sampled.

parameters.name: normalmap
parameters.label: Normal Map (Bump)
parameters.description: Uses a from TOPs to create a 'bump map' effect. Bump-mapping simulates bumps or wrinkles in a surface to give it a 3D depth effect.

parameters.name: normalmapsamplingmode
parameters.label: Texture Sampling Mode
parameters.description: Mode to pick how the texture is to be sampled.

parameters.name: bumpscale
parameters.label: Bump Scale
parameters.description: A multiplier for the 'bump effect' created by the Normal Map parameter.

parameters.name: heightmap
parameters.label: Height Map
parameters.description: Specifies a height texture map. The height map is used in conjunction with the normal map to perform parallax mapping.

parameters.name: heightmapsamplingmode
parameters.label: Texture Sampling Mode
parameters.description: Mode to pick how the texture is to be sampled.

parameters.name: parallaxscale
parameters.label: Parallax Scale
parameters.description: Scale value applied to the height map. Can be used to increase or exaggerate the effect.

parameters.name: parallaxocclusion
parameters.label: Parallax Occlusion
parameters.description: Enables parallax occlusion, an enhancement of the parallax mapping technique used with the height map. Parallax occlusion improves the quality of the texture offsetting in parallax mapping so that the

parameters.name: displaceverts
parameters.label: Displace Vertices
parameters.description: When Enable Height Map above is On, setting Displace to On will enable true displacement mapping where the vertices of the geometry are displaced based on the Height Map texture and the parameters bel

parameters.name: displacescale
parameters.label: Displace Scale
parameters.description: A multiplier for the displacement amount.

parameters.name: displacemid
parameters.label: Displace Midpoint
parameters.description: Sets the middle point of displacement map as the start position for the displacement effect.

parameters.name: diffusemap
parameters.label: Diffuse Map
parameters.description: Specifies a that multiples the Diffuse Color. The object must have texture coordinates. The alpha of this map is ignored.

parameters.name: diffusemapsamplingmode
parameters.label: Texture Sampling Mode
parameters.description: Mode to pick how the texture is to be sampled.

parameters.name: specmap
parameters.label: Specular Map
parameters.description: Specifies a texture that is multiplied with the Specular color parameter of the material. The object must have texture coordinates. The alpha of this map is ignored.

parameters.name: specmapsamplingmode
parameters.label: Texture Sampling Mode
parameters.description: Mode to pick how the texture is to be sampled.

parameters.name: emitmap
parameters.label: Emit Map
parameters.description: Specifies a texture that is multiplied with the Emit color parameter of the material. The object must have texture coordinates. The alpha of this map is ignored.

parameters.name: emitmapsamplingmode
parameters.label: Texture Sampling Mode
parameters.description: Mode to pick how the texture is to be sampled.

parameters.name: envmap
parameters.label: Environment Map
parameters.description: Uses a texture to define an environment map for the material. Environment mapping simulates an object reflecting its surroundings. The defined in this parameter is the texture that will be reflected.

parameters.name: envmaptype2d
parameters.label: Environment Map 2D Type
parameters.description: Select between using a sphere map or an equirectangular map as the Environment Map type.

parameters.name: frontfacelit
parameters.label: Polygon Front Faces
parameters.description: Controls how the polygon's normal is used to light the front face of the polygon. For more information refer to the article.

parameters.name: backfacelit
parameters.label: Polygon Back Faces
parameters.description: Controls how the polygon's normal is used to light the back face of the polygon. For more information refer to the article.

parameters.name: outputshader
parameters.label: Output Shader...
parameters.description: This button will bring up a dialog that will create a and with shader code that matches whatever effect this Phong is currently creating. Since shaders are dependent on the number and type of lights,

parameters.name: alphamap
parameters.label: Alpha Map
parameters.description: This map multiplies the alpha of the object. It uses the red channel of the map, other channels are ignored.

parameters.name: alphamapsamplingmode
parameters.label: Texture Sampling Mode
parameters.description: Mode to pick how the texture is to be sampled.

parameters.name: alphamode
parameters.label: Uniform Alpha
parameters.description: Turning this off will make the alpha change depending on orientation of each polygon's normal compared to the camera. Normals that are pointing at the camera will results in the polygon having an alph

parameters.name: alphafront
parameters.label: Alpha Front
parameters.description: The opacity of the material. This parameter is multiplied by point alpha of the object (as will as any other alpha source).

parameters.name: alphaside
parameters.label: Alpha Side
parameters.description: This is used for non-uniform alpha. It is the alpha value polygons that are facing away from the camera will get.

parameters.name: rolloff
parameters.label: Alpha Rolloff
parameters.description: Controls how the alpha changes from Alpha Front to Alpha Side.

parameters.name: postmultalpha
parameters.label: Post-Mult Color by Alpha
parameters.description: At the end of all of the calculations, the color (RGB) is multiplied by the calculated alpha. You can stop this from happening by turning off this toggle.

parameters.name: alphamultlight
parameters.label: Mult Alpha by Light Luminance
parameters.description: When this is enabled, the luminance of the lighting will be multiplied by the alpha, to decrease/increase it.

parameters.name: multitexturing
parameters.label: Multi-Texturing (Disables Color Map)
parameters.description: Enables multi-texturing. This disables the Color Map parameter.

parameters.name: texture1
parameters.label: Texture 1
parameters.description: You can specify up to 4 textures for multi-texturing.

parameters.name: texture1samplingmode
parameters.label: Texture Sampling Mode
parameters.description: Mode to pick how the texture is to be sampled.

parameters.name: texture1coord
parameters.label: SOP Texture Coord
parameters.description: Specifies which texture coordinate to use for the map.

parameters.name: texture2
parameters.label: Texture 2
parameters.description: You can specify up to 4 textures for multi-texturing.

parameters.name: texture2samplingmode
parameters.label: Texture Sampling Mode
parameters.description: Mode to pick how the texture is to be sampled.

parameters.name: texture2coord
parameters.label: SOP Texture Coord
parameters.description: Specifies which texture coordinate to use for the map.

parameters.name: texture3
parameters.label: Texture 3
parameters.description: You can specify up to 4 textures for multi-texturing.

parameters.name: texture3samplingmode
parameters.label: Texture Sampling Mode
parameters.description: Mode to pick how the texture is to be sampled.

parameters.name: texture3coord
parameters.label: SOP Texture Coord
parameters.description: Specifies which texture coordinate to use for the map.

parameters.name: texture4
parameters.label: Texture 4
parameters.description: You can specify up to 4 textures for multi-texturing.

parameters.name: texture4samplingmode
parameters.label: Texture Sampling Mode
parameters.description: Mode to pick how the texture is to be sampled.

parameters.name: texture4coord
parameters.label: SOP Texture Coord
parameters.description: Specifies which texture coordinate to use for the map.

parameters.name: multitexexpr
parameters.label: GLSL Expression
parameters.description: GLSL code that combines the texture images (look to the start of this section for more details). This parameter can be left blank (which means the maps will just be multiplied together).

parameters.name: rimlight0map
parameters.label: Color Map
parameters.description: This map will multiple the calculated rim light color.

parameters.name: rimlight0mapsamplingmode
parameters.label: Texture Sampling Mode
parameters.description: Mode to pick how the texture is to be sampled.

parameters.name: rimlight0center
parameters.label: Center
parameters.description: The center of the rim lights location, situated somewhere on a 360 degree circle.

parameters.name: rimlight0width
parameters.label: Width
parameters.description: How far from the center the rim light extends.

parameters.name: rimlight0strength
parameters.label: Strength
parameters.description: Controls the brightness of the rim light.

parameters.name: rimlight0strengthramp
parameters.label: Strength Ramp
parameters.description: You can specify a horizontal ramp (it will sample the texture at v = 0.5), which controls the the rim lights strength.

parameters.name: shadowstrength
parameters.label: Shadow Strength
parameters.description: This parameter will control how much being in a shadow will change the color of the lighting. At 1 the object will take on the parameter, at 0 it will behave as if it's not in a shadow, even if it is.

parameters.name: darknessemit
parameters.label: Darkness Emit
parameters.description: The Phong calculates the current brightness of color of the objects, after taking into account lights, rim lights, emission etc. It then uses this brightness (between 0-1) and fades in the . The darke

parameters.name: darknessemitmap
parameters.label: Darkness Emit Map
parameters.description: This map multiplies the . This maps alpha is not used.

parameters.name: darknessemitmapsamplingmode
parameters.label: Texture Sampling Mode
parameters.description: Mode to pick how the texture is to be sampled.

parameters.name: shininess2
parameters.label: Secondary Shininess
parameters.description: Controls the secondary specular highlights (glossyness) of an object. Higher settings are more glossy, like plastic or shiny metal. Lower settings give more of a matte finish.

parameters.name: writecameradepthtoalpha
parameters.label: Write Camera Space Depth to Alpha
parameters.description: This causes the camera space depth of the pixel to be written to the alpha channel of the output . This value can be useful for post-processing effects, but ofcourse you will not have the result of al

parameters.name: applypointcolor
parameters.label: Apply Point Color
parameters.description: Normally the color attribute (Cd[4]) coming from the is used in the lighting calculation, you can turn off using the color attribute by un-checking this parameter.

parameters.name: instancetexture
parameters.label: Instance Texture
parameters.description: When provider per-instance textures in the , this parameter selects which map the instance texture will be applied as.

parameters.name: color0output
parameters.label: RGB
parameters.description: Allows sending things like normals or diffuse color to different Render color buffers in a single pass.

parameters.name: deformdata
parameters.label: Get Bone Data:
parameters.description: Specifies where the deform bone data will be obtained.

parameters.name: targetsop
parameters.label: SOP with Capture Data
parameters.description: Specifies the that contains the deform capture attributes.

parameters.name: pcaptpath
parameters.label: pCaptPath Attrib
parameters.description: Specifies the name of the pCaptPath attribute to use. When your geometry has been put through a , the attributes will be split into names like pCaptPath0, pCaptPath1. You can only render 1 bone group

parameters.name: skelrootpath
parameters.label: Skeleton Root Path
parameters.description: Specifies the path to the where the root of the skeleton is located.

parameters.name: mat
parameters.label: MAT
parameters.description: When obtaining deform data from a or a Deform In , this is where that is specified.

parameters.name: blending
parameters.label: Blending (Transparency)
parameters.description: This toggle enables and disables blending. However see the wiki article .

parameters.name: srcblend
parameters.label: Source Color *
parameters.description: This value is multiplied by the color value of the pixel that is being written to the Color-Buffer (also know as the Source Color).

parameters.name: destblend
parameters.label: Destination Color *
parameters.description: This value is multiplied by the color value of the pixel currently in the Color-Buffer (also known as the Destination Color).

parameters.name: separatealphafunc
parameters.label: Separate Alpha Function
parameters.description: This toggle enables and disables separate blending options for the alpha values.

parameters.name: srcblenda
parameters.label: Source Alpha *
parameters.description: This value is multiplied by the alpha value of the pixel that is being written to the Color-Buffer (also know as the Source Alpha).

parameters.name: destblenda
parameters.label: Destination Alpha *
parameters.description: This value is multiplied by the alpha value of the pixel currently in the Color-Buffer (also known as the Destination Alpha).

parameters.name: depthtest
parameters.label: Depth Test
parameters.description: Enables and disables the Depth-Test. If the depth-test is disabled, depths values aren't written to the Depth-Buffer.

parameters.name: depthfunc
parameters.label: Depth Test Function
parameters.description: The depth value of the pixel being drawn is compared to the depth value currently in the depth-buffer using this function. If the test passes then the pixel is drawn to the Buffer. If the test fails t

parameters.name: depthwriting
parameters.label: Write Depth Values
parameters.description: If Write Depth Values is on, pixels that pass the depth-test will write their depth value to the Depth-Buffer. If this isn't on then no changes will be made to the Depth-Buffer, regardless of if the p

parameters.name: alphatest
parameters.label: Discard Pixels Based on Alpha
parameters.description: This enables or disables the pixel alpha test.

parameters.name: alphafunc
parameters.label: Keep Pixels with Alpha
parameters.description: This menu works in conjunction with the Alpha Threshold parameter below in determining which pixels to keep based on their alpha value.

parameters.name: alphathreshold
parameters.label: Alpha Threshold
parameters.description: This value is what the pixel's alpha is compared to to determine if the pixel should be drawn. Pixels with alpha greater than the Alpha Threshold will be drawn. Pixels with alpha less than or equal to

parameters.name: wireframe
parameters.label: Wire Frame
parameters.description: Enables and disables wire-frame rendering with the option of OpenGL Tesselated or Topology based wireframes.

parameters.name: wirewidth
parameters.label: Line Width
parameters.description: This value is the width that the wires will be. This value is in pixels.

parameters.name: polygonoffset
parameters.label: Polygon Depth Offset
parameters.description: Turns on the polygon offset feature.

parameters.name: shadowcolorb
parameters.label: Shadowcolorb
parameters.description: The color that will be used in shadowed areas.

parameters.name: ambb
parameters.label: Ambb
parameters.description: The color of the ambient light reflected from the material.

parameters.name: emitb
parameters.label: Emitb
parameters.description: This is the color that the material will emit even if there is no light.

parameters.name: envmaprotatez
parameters.label: Envmaprotatez
parameters.description: Rotate the texture specified by the Environment Map parameter above.

parameters.name: darknessemitcolorb
parameters.label: Darknessemitcolorb
parameters.description: The color that is used for areas that are in darkness.

parameters.name: diffb
parameters.label: Diffb
parameters.description: The color of the diffuse light reflected from the material.

parameters.name: spec2b
parameters.label: Spec2B
parameters.description: Adds a secondary specular highlight color.

parameters.name: specb
parameters.label: Specb
parameters.description: The color of the specular light reflected from the material. This changes the color of the highlights on shiney objects.

parameters.name: constantb
parameters.label: Constantb
parameters.description: Adds to the final color. Where there are point colors, finalcolor += Point Color * Constant Color. This behaves like there is ambient illumination of 1 1 1. It is not affected by textures or transpare

parameters.name: envmapcolorb
parameters.label: Envmapcolorb
parameters.description: This color is multiplied with the texture specified by the Environment Map parameter above.
